subject: Update vfc charts configutation
commit_message:
Update vfc charts configutation

Change-Id: I79dc1c5d26692fe675ca4cf2779fc78eac557a45
Issue-ID: VFC-879
Signed-off-by: yangyan <yangyanyj@chinamobile.com>

entries.message:
Patch Set 4: Code-Review+1

Seems good to me.

Thx,
Mahendra

Patch Set 4: Code-Review-1 Verified-1

Too long name for the service.

Error: release aaa failed: Deployment.apps "aaa-vfc-huawei-vnfm-driver" is invalid: [spec.template.spec.containers[0].ports[0].name: Invalid value: "vfc-huawei-vnfm-driver": must be no more than 15 characters, spec.template.spec.containers[0].ports[1].name: Invalid value: "vfc-huawei-vnfm-driver2": must be no more than 15 characters]

Patch Set 4:

@Borislav Glozman, Is this a new requiring that the length of the port name must be no more than 15 characters? I just want to confirm that because  several vfc components port name is more than 15 characters and the previous verification has never found this problem.If this is the new requirements, I will fix all the port name.

Patch Set 4:

> @Borislav Glozman, Is this a new requiring that the length of the
 > port name must be no more than 15 characters? I just want to
 > confirm that because  several vfc components port name is more than
 > 15 characters and the previous verification has never found this
 > problem.If this is the new requirements, I will fix all the port
 > name.

I do not think it is a new requirement, it is the error that I received when deployed this patch on rancher k8s. It does not pass verification.

Patch Set 5: Verified-1

Error: release deep failed: Deployment.apps "deep-vfc-huawei-vnfm-driver" is invalid: [spec.template.spec.containers[0].ports[0].name: Invalid value: "HW-Http": must contain only alpha-numeric characters (a-z, 0-9), and hyphens (-), spec.template.spec.containers[0].ports[1].name: Invalid value: "HW-Https": must contain only alpha-numeric characters (a-z, 0-9), and hyphens (-)]

Patch Set 6: Code-Review+1 Verified+1

It makes, lints and launches however many of the containers are crashing out.  Looking at some logs they are trying to talk to a mariadb somewhere.  Some are looking for it at their own pod IP:3306 and some are looking for it at localhost:3306

I checked the state before and after this patch and it is about the same so I think this patch isn't the cause.

Patch Set 6:

To add to Mandeep's previous comment, all charts should use the service name for mariadb so that the pods can resolve to the correct host when deployed in a multi-node k8s cluster. On a single kubernetes host the pods come up successfully.

Patch Set 6:

hi Mahendra and Mike, all crashed component contain a mysql database in their docker image. that's why they are looking for database at their own pod IP:3306 and localhost:3306. How do you suggest that we fix this issue.Should we set the database ip to the service name of their component?

Patch Set 6:

> hi Mahendra and Mike, all crashed component contain a mysql
 > database in their docker image. that's why they are looking for
 > database at their own pod IP:3306 and localhost:3306. How do you
 > suggest that we fix this issue.Should we set the database ip to the
 > service name of their component?

Hi Yan,

The issue appears when VFC is deployed in a multi-node kubernetes cluster. The databases do not seem to come up.

[33] 20 Apr 17:53:44.794 # Server started, Redis version 2.8.4
[33] 20 Apr 17:53:44.794 * The server is now ready to accept connections on port 6379
 * Starting MySQL database server mysqld
   ...fail!
start create nfvocatalog db
ERROR 2003 (HY000): Can't connect to MySQL server on '127.0.0.1' (111)
failed to create nfvocatalog database
Unhandled exception in thread started by <function wrapper at 0x7f33a1ed2ed8>
. . . .
django.db.utils.OperationalError: (2003, "Can't connect to MySQL server on '127.0.0.1' ([Errno 111] Connection refused)”)

Not sure why they are failing, needs to be investigated. There are other pods that are also failing in a multi-node environment. These have been traced back to communication-related issues, like not using the service names for hostname resolution.  If you discover this is a communication issue and need the ip assigned to the pod, this can be made available through environment variables and or configuration.

If you and Mahendra need a working environment in the open lab to test with, please look at this guide to set one up https://wiki.onap.org/display/DW/ONAP+on+Windriver+ONAP+Developer+Cloud. Or ping me and I can build one for you.
Change has been successfully merged by Mandeep Khinda
